evidence.observed: Public run had 120/120 rows with status=wrapper_error and null answers; sealed telemetry indicated Connection error from provider=local.

findings.root_cause: The local model endpoint was not reachable from the simulator process, most likely LOCAL_BASE_URL was unset or not exported into the shell running the Linux binary.
findings.suggested_fix: Export LOCAL_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1 before running sim/score; wrapper now logs call_next exceptions and config uses longer timeout plus three retry attempts.

evidence.observed: Initial prompt only said to help and give a VND total; temperature was 1.6 and verify was false, so exact subtotal/discount/shipping arithmetic was not constrained.

findings.root_cause: The model was asked for a total without an exact formula or verification step, while high temperature made arithmetic unstable.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt now defines the exact formula and config lowers temperature and enables verify.

evidence.observed: Initial prompt did not distinguish order notes from instructions; wrapper did not sanitize note text before calling the agent.

findings.root_cause: User-supplied notes could override stock, price, discount, or privacy instructions.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt treats notes as data only; wrapper neutralizes common instruction phrases inside note sections.

evidence.observed: Initial config had redact_pii=false and wrapper returned raw answers.

findings.root_cause: No privacy instruction or output redaction guarded emails, phone numbers, card numbers, or IDs.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt forbids repeating PII; config enables redact_pii; wrapper redacts answer text and logs pii_in_answer.

evidence.observed: Initial tool_budget was 0 and loop_guard was false, allowing repeated or unnecessary tool calls.

findings.root_cause: The prompt lacked a one-call-per-tool rule and config did not cap tool use.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt caps each needed tool at once; config enables loop_guard and sets tool_budget to 3.

evidence.observed: Initial prompt asked the agent to help and give a total in VND without requiring stock/tool grounding or a no-total refusal path.

findings.root_cause: The model could invent totals for unknown, unsupported, or out-of-stock orders because refusal conditions were absent.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt now requires check_stock grounding and refuses unknown/out-of-stock/unsupported cases without a total.

evidence.observed: Initial normalize_unicode=false and catalog_override marked macbook out of stock, while public traffic includes diacritic city names and MacBook orders.

findings.root_cause: Unicode/city normalization failures and catalog overrides can make valid tool lookups fail or return contradictory stock data.
findings.suggested_fix: Config enables normalize_unicode and clears catalog_override.

evidence.observed: Initial session_drift_rate was 0.06 with no context reset, allowing later turns in a session to degrade.

findings.root_cause: Stateful context drift can corrupt repeated user sessions and coupon/order handling.
findings.suggested_fix: Config sets session_drift_rate to 0 and context_reset_every to 4.

evidence.observed: Initial verbose_system=true, context_size=8, max_completion_tokens=2000, and no tool budget made the agent unnecessarily expensive.

findings.root_cause: Large context/output limits and verbose prompting inflate token use without improving simple order calculations.
findings.suggested_fix: Config disables verbose_system, reduces context/output limits, and caps tool calls.
